commit.message:
feat: DevTunnel, snapfeed feedback, bandwidth stats, Gastown, Go TUI

## DevTunnel support
- Named persistent tunnels (stable URL across restarts)
- Auto-detection: prefers devtunnel, falls back to cloudflare
- CLI: --tunnel-provider auto|devtunnel|cloudflare

## Snapfeed UI feedback (@microsoft/snapfeed)
- GitHub Device Flow auth (user's own identity)
- Server-side token storage (~/.remux/github-token)
- GitHub OAuth proxy endpoints (CORS bypass)
- Telemetry endpoint: POST /api/telemetry/events (SQLite)
- Issues include: message, context, console errors, network log,
  session replay, screenshot (opt-in)

## Server extensions
- Terminal state tracker (@xterm/headless, mosh-like diffs)
- Bandwidth stats (rolling window, RTT, diff tracking)
- Push notifications (VAPID, bell/exit detection)
- Gastown workspace detection + session enrichment
- Structured event watcher (events.jsonl)
- File browser API (GET /api/files)

## Go TUI client (tui/)
- Multi-host terminal client (bubbletea + lipgloss)
- Client-side scrollback cache
- 6 unit + 2 integration tests

## start.ps1
- Stable token across restarts (~/.remux/token)
- --prod / dev mode with proper flag forwarding

Fix movement: use frame-step walk model for wall-safe movement

Replace target-tile model with a step-based loop that consumes movement budget
each frame. Direction changes and wall checks only happen when exactly at a tile
center (integer snap point), making it physically impossible to enter a wall.

feat: add Vitest test environment and CI test job (#21)

No automated tests or test infrastructure existed; CI only ran type
checks and builds.

## Changes

- **`vitest.config.ts`** — new Vitest config with `jsdom` environment
for browser API access (`localStorage`, `Notification`)
- **`src/storage.test.ts`** — 16 unit tests covering all CRUD functions
including `notified` reset logic in `updateProduct`
- **`src/notifications.test.ts`** — 9 unit tests for
`getDaysUntilExpiry` (boundary cases) and `checkAndNotify` (permission
guard, threshold logic, deduplication via `notified` flag)
- **`package.json`** — adds `"test": "vitest run"` script
- **`tsconfig.json`** — includes `vitest.config.ts` in compilation scope
- **`.github/workflows/ci.yml`** — adds `test` job running `npm test`
alongside existing `typecheck` and `build` jobs

Module mocking uses `vi.mock` hoisted at the top level; `Notification`
global is stubbed per-test via `vi.stubGlobal`:

```ts
vi.mock('./storage.ts', () => ({
  loadProducts: vi.fn(),
  markNotified: vi.fn(),
}));

beforeEach(() => {
  const MockNotification = vi.fn();
  Object.defineProperty(MockNotification, 'permission', { value: 'granted', writable: true, configurable: true });
  vi.stubGlobal('Notification', MockNotification);
});
```

commit.message:
fix: edit modal save closes window and shows save feedback (#20)

The save button in the edit modal had no error boundary — if
`updateProduct` or `renderProductList` threw (e.g. localStorage quota
exceeded), `cleanup()` was never reached and the modal stayed open.
There was also no confirmation that the save succeeded.

## Changes

- **`showToast(message, type)`** — new helper that appends a temporary
`role="status"` / `aria-live="polite"` div at the top of the screen,
fades out after 3 s
- **`onSave` wrapped in try/catch:**
- Success → `cleanup()` closes modal, then green `✅ Produkt gespeichert`
toast
- Error → inline `edit-error-msg` shows `"Fehler beim Speichern. Bitte
versuche es erneut."`, modal stays open for retry

```ts
try {
  updateProduct(product.id, { name, expiryDate, notifyDaysBefore });
  renderProductList(container);
  checkAndNotify();
  cleanup();
  showToast('✅ Produkt gespeichert');
} catch {
  if (errorMsg) errorMsg.textContent = 'Fehler beim Speichern. Bitte versuche es erneut.';
}
```

## Screenshot

![Modal open before
save](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/14683926-a0ae-4c6a-bfa2-729bd1a79b5d)
![Modal closed with success
toast](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/7f085375-e2f1-4f06-bef8-ce18b86dd2e1)

commit.message:
fix: agent clones stale feature branch instead of newest version from push target (#825)

* Initial plan

* fix: agent mode clones issue branch instead of requested branch

- IssueWorker: when feature branch exists on Working (push target) remote,
  switch clone source to Working to ensure newest version is used and
  avoid 'fetch first' push rejections
- DockerAgentRuntime: when push fails, automatically fetch from push
  target, rebase local commits, and retry push; if rebase has conflicts,
  abort and emit push-failed marker for downstream handling

Co-authored-by: m-s-work <122795841+m-s-work@users.noreply.github.com>
Agent-Logs-Url: https://github.com/issuepit/issuepit/sessions/6d7d0233-4610-450c-a485-af6ea2de335e

* fix: correct dead-code retried flag in push retry logic

The `retried` flag was set to true only alongside `pushSucceeded = true`,
making the `!retried` guard inside `if (!pushSucceeded)` always evaluate
to true — it never suppressed the generic failure message it was meant to.

Replace with `specificFailureLogged` which is set to true whenever a
more specific failure reason (rebase conflicts, retry push failed) was
already logged, so the generic "Push failed (allowed — ...)" message is
correctly suppressed in those cases.

Co-authored-by: m-s-work <122795841+m-s-work@users.noreply.github.com>
Agent-Logs-Url: https://github.com/issuepit/issuepit/sessions/a9ea51b7-a242-4482-8bcf-3aee4b33af87

* fix: count feature branch commits across all remotes, loop all remotes on push failure

Clone source selection:
- Replace the guess-Working-is-newest approach with a blobless partial clone
  (--filter=blob:none) per remote to count commits on the feature branch;
  the remote with the highest count is selected as the clone source.
- New CountBranchCommitsOnRemoteAsync static helper handles the clone+count
  and cleans up the temp directory in a finally block.

Push failure recovery:
- Add TryIntegrateRemotesAndRetryPushAsync to IExecCapableRuntime (interface)
  and implement in DockerAgentRuntime.
- The method loops through ALL configured remotes sequentially: fetch branch →
  rebase FETCH_HEAD → abort+fail on conflicts; after all clean rebases it
  retries the push and emits an updated GIT_COMMIT_SHA marker on success.
- IssueWorker calls TryIntegrateRemotesAndRetryPushAsync after capturedGitPushFailed
  before falling through to the .git archive upload / session failure path.
- EmitGitMarkersAsync simplified: just push → emit GIT_PUSH_FAILED on failure;
  the single-Working-only recovery is superseded by the full loop in IssueWorker.

Also fix unobserved async drain tasks in CountBranchCommitsOnRemoteAsync:
store stderr/stdout ReadToEndAsync tasks and await them with Task.WhenAll/await
to prevent deadlocks when process buffers fill and to ensure unobserved exceptions
do not escape silently.

Fix rough SVG-to-G-code curve linearization (#42)

* Initial plan

* Initial plan for rough SVG to G-code conversion fix

Co-authored-by: praegustator <2522478+praegustator@users.noreply.github.com>
Agent-Logs-Url: https://github.com/praegustator/laserflow/sessions/4e0373b3-a448-4299-9c3e-0c52480b79b4

* Fix rough SVG-to-G-code conversion: reduce curve tolerance and make linearization scale-aware

The curve linearization was computing segment counts based on raw SVG
coordinate space without accounting for the layer transform scale.  When
curves were scaled up (e.g. 2×–5×), the segment count stayed the same,
producing visibly rough output with gaps up to 2.4mm between points.

Two changes:
1. Reduce CURVE_TOLERANCE from 0.5mm to 0.1mm for finer default quality.
2. Multiply the control polygon length by the transform's max scale factor
   before computing segment count, so the tolerance is enforced in the
   output (mm) space rather than the SVG coordinate space.

Result: max gap between consecutive points is now consistently ~0.1mm
regardless of transform scale.

commit.message:
Add Verilog HDL model skeleton for Verilator simulation

Phase 8.1: Project setup for RISC-V emulator Verilog implementation

RTL modules:
- emulator_top.v: Top-level wrapper with control/memory interfaces
- cpu.v: RV32IF CPU core (single-cycle, full instruction set)
- alu.v: Integer ALU (ADD, SUB, AND, OR, XOR, shifts, comparisons)
- fpu.v: FP unit using DPI-C for IEEE 754 operations
- memory.v: Dual-port memory with byte/half/word access

Testbench:
- verilator_runner.cpp: CLI-compatible runner matching emulator_runner
- DPI-C functions for FPU operations (add, sub, mul, div, cmp, cvt)
- ELF loader and framebuffer extraction

Build system:
- Makefile for standalone Verilator build
- CMakeLists.txt for CMake integration (optional)

Add POSIX keystring parsing and argv pre-expansion (#114)

Support traditional key syntax (for example `tar cvf archive.tar file`)
by rewriting a leading key operand into clap-compatible short options
before argument parsing.

- detect valid keystrings in argv[1]
- expand key letters into `-<opt>` arguments
- consume operands for `f` and `b` in key order
- forward `-b` (currently unsupported) so key/dash forms fail consistently
- update usage text to document key syntax
- add unit tests for key detection/expansion
- add integration tests for create/extract parity and `-b` failure parity

Fix rough SVG to G-code conversion: handle arc commands, adaptive curve linearization (#33)

* Initial plan

* Fix rough SVG to G-code conversion: handle arc commands and use adaptive curve linearization

Two root causes fixed:
1. SVG arc commands (A) were silently dropped during G-code generation.
   Circles and ellipses parsed by SvgParser produce arc commands, but
   GcodeGenerator had no handler for them. Added A_TO_C and NORMALIZE_ST
   transformers to convert arcs and smooth curves to cubic beziers before
   processing.

2. Bezier curve linearization used a fixed 10 segments regardless of curve
   size. A 1mm curve and a 100mm curve both got 10 segments, causing
   visible faceting on larger curves. Replaced with adaptive segment count
   based on control polygon length (0.5mm tolerance).
